text.title: aws-ec2-provisioning Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for aws-ec2-provisioning. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: All EC2 instances MUST require IMDSv2 (HttpTokens=required, HttpPutResponseHopLimit=2) and use encrypted gp3 EBS root volumes. No instance MUST be launched with a key pair if SSM Session Manager is available — attach an IAM instance profile with AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore instead.
rule.rationale: IMDSv1 is vulnerable to SSRF credential theft. Encrypted EBS is free on gp3 and mandated by PCI-DSS, SOC 2, and HIPAA. SSM Session Manager provides audited access without managing SSH keys or opening port 22.

rule.statement: Create a launch template for every ASG. The template MUST encode: ImageId, InstanceType, IamInstanceProfile, MetadataOptions (IMDSv2), BlockDeviceMappings (gp3 encrypted), and SecurityGroupIds. Never hardcode UserData credentials — reference Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store from within the instance instead.
rule.rationale: Launch templates make ASG instance refresh zero-downtime and reproducible. Hardcoded credentials in UserData appear in the EC2 console and CloudTrail logs in plaintext.

rule.statement: Auto Scaling Groups MUST use health-check type ELB (not EC2) when a load balancer target group is attached, spread across at least 2 Availability Zones, and apply a target tracking scaling policy on ASGAverageCPUUtilization at 70%. Use instance refresh with min_healthy_percentage=75 for zero-downtime AMI rollouts.
rule.rationale: EC2 health checks only detect OS-level failures; ELB health checks detect application-level failures. CPU target tracking at 70% leaves headroom for traffic spikes while preventing over-provisioning. Instance refresh eliminates manual draining and re-registration during AMI updates.

rule.statement: Create one security group per application tier (ALB, app, database). App tier ingress MUST reference the ALB security group ID, database tier ingress MUST reference the app security group ID — never use CIDR blocks for inter-tier traffic. Add descriptions to every rule. Restrict SSH/RDP to specific IP ranges; never allow 0.0.0.0/0 on port 22 or 3389.
rule.rationale: Security-group-to-security-group references automatically track instance IP changes without rule updates. CIDR-based rules drift as subnets expand. Descriptions are required for audit trails and are enforced by some compliance frameworks.
